metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4511
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Amounts of anti-GluR2/3 antibody-immunoreactive AMPA receptor proteins were slightly increased in the mutant mice (p =0.005, Fig. 2A). The number of gold particles of AMPA receptors per profile of parallel fiber-Purkinje cell (PF-PC) synapses in the Delphilin mutant mice (6.460.3, n = 300 from 3 mice) was significantly larger than that in the wild-type mice (Fig. 2F).
Fig 3: Electrophysiological analysis of PF-PC synapses: Normal synaptic transmission in delphilin mutants as compared to WT. LTD was induced by 200 ms depolarization of a PC to -20 mV coupled with paired PF stimuli applied at 15 and 65 ms after the onset of depolarization. This conjunctive stimulation was repeated once, twice, 5X, 10X and 20X. Delphilin-deficient mutants displayed a much more efficient LTD after 2 and 5 conjunctions (Fig. 3A-E).
EGTA experiments suggest that long-term depression (LTD) requires lower Ca2+ levels in Delphilin KO animals (Fig. 4B). Experimental description: LTD induction was facilitated at PF-PC synapses in Delphilin mutant mice (page 1, e2297). And: ...backcrossed 5 times to theC57BL/6 strain,
